kp.name: Kp Index
kp.quick: A measure of geomagnetic disturbance on a 0-9 scale.
kp.full: The planetary K-index (Kp) is a global index derived from magnetometer records at multiple observatories. It summarizes short-term geomagnetic variability.
kp.formula: Kp is derived from local K-indices and standardized to a quasi-logarithmic global scale.
kp.history: Introduced by Julius Bartels and collaborators in the mid-20th century.
kp.context: Kp 0-2 quiet, Kp 5 minor storm, Kp 8-9 severe to extreme.
kp.citation: Bartels, J. (1949). Ann. Intern. Geophys. Year.

bz.name: IMF Bz
bz.quick: North-south magnetic component of the interplanetary field.
bz.full: When Bz is negative (southward), coupling with Earth's magnetic field is stronger and storms are more likely.
bz.formula: Commonly interpreted with coupling functions such as Newell dPhi/dt.
bz.history: Space physics usage expanded during the space age from in-situ IMF measurements.
bz.context: Sustained Bz below -10 nT often signals elevated geomagnetic risk.
